$meta.step: Step 2 — Design System and UX Foundation
$meta.description: Sizing scale, including the non-negotiable 48x48 minimum touch target.

size.touch.min.description: Minimum interactive target on every touch surface, in logical pixels. Applies to buttons, list rows, checkboxes, chips, icon buttons and any tappable region — including targets whose painted area is smaller, which must still expose a 48x48 hit area.
size.touch.min.accessibilityNotes: Non-negotiable. A target below 48x48 is an accessibility defect, not a density choice.
prohibitedUsage: Never reduced to fit more content on screen.

size.touch.spacing.min.description: Minimum gap between two adjacent touch targets, so a mis-tap does not trigger the wrong action.

size.pointer.min.description: Minimum interactive target on pointer-driven Console Web surfaces, where a 48px target would waste dense table space. Console Web rows still expose a 32px minimum row height.

size.icon.sm.description: Icon box size for the sm role. An icon is never the only carrier of meaning.

size.icon.md.description: Icon box size for the md role. An icon is never the only carrier of meaning.

size.icon.lg.description: Icon box size for the lg role. An icon is never the only carrier of meaning.

size.icon.xl.description: Icon box size for the xl role. An icon is never the only carrier of meaning.

size.control.sm.description: Control height for the sm role. On touch surfaces only control.lg and control.xl may be used for primary interactive controls, because they satisfy size.touch.min.

size.control.md.description: Control height for the md role. On touch surfaces only control.lg and control.xl may be used for primary interactive controls, because they satisfy size.touch.min.

size.control.lg.description: Control height for the lg role. On touch surfaces only control.lg and control.xl may be used for primary interactive controls, because they satisfy size.touch.min.

size.control.xl.description: Control height for the xl role. On touch surfaces only control.lg and control.xl may be used for primary interactive controls, because they satisfy size.touch.min.

size.maxWidth.content.description: Maximum content width on wide Console Web layouts before the container centres.

size.maxWidth.reading.description: Maximum width of a long-form reading column.

size.appbar.height.description: App bar height on the Android surfaces.

size.appbar.height.web.description: App bar height on Console Web, taller to accommodate the tenant and outlet context bar.

size.bottomnav.height.description: Bottom navigation height. Exceeds size.touch.min so each destination clears the touch floor with its label.

size.navrail.width.description: Navigation rail width at the medium breakpoint and above.

size.sidenav.width.description: Expanded side navigation width on Console Web.

size.sidenav.width.collapsed.description: Collapsed side navigation width, still wide enough for an icon target and its focus ring.

size.bottomsheet.max.description: Maximum bottom sheet width; beyond this the sheet centres rather than stretching.

size.dialog.width.sm.description: Narrow dialog width for a single confirmation.

size.row.compact.description: Compact table row height. Pointer-only: it sits below size.touch.min and is never used on a touch surface.
prohibitedUsage: Never used on Customer Android, Ops Android or the Public Tracking Portal, because 40px is below the 48dp touch floor.
size.row.compact.accessibilityNotes: Pointer-only. On a touch surface the standard density row height applies instead.

size.avatar.sm.description: Small avatar diameter, used inside dense lists and tables.

size.avatar.lg.description: Large avatar diameter, used on profile headers.

size.icon.xs.description: Extra-small icon box, used only inside a dense chip or an inline badge. Never a tap target on its own.
prohibitedUsage: Never used as a standalone interactive target — a 12px icon must sit inside a control that satisfies size.touch.min.

size.control.xs.description: Extra-small control height for pointer-driven Console Web affordances such as an inline table filter chip.
prohibitedUsage: Never used on a touch surface — 24px is far below the 48dp touch floor.
